title: Copper output in Zambia could jump 10%, mines minister says
impact_evidence: Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "Zambia is close to resolving a dispute with mining companies over electricity tariffs that has dogged the industry for three years. Yaluma said he expects an agreement to be reached in the coming weeks, but declined to elaborate." | "Zesco, which produces almost all of Zambia's power, agreed to cut charges for mine operators, in March, while proposing to raise them by half for other customers. Miners' electricity costs will be 9.3 US cents per kilowatt-hour, lower than the 10.35c it raised tariffs to at the start of 2016, it said."

title: A brighter outlook for Zambia's copper sector
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "Indian mining company Vedanta Resources announced plans last month to invest US$1bn in its Zambian unit, Konkola Copper Mines (KCM), creating 7,000 jobs." | "It also employs nearly 10,000 people." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Unemployment risks post-closure -> "During that time, a number of companies were forced to downscale their investments, while others placed mines in "care and maintenance"." | "Companies such as FQM and KCM had earlier announced the suspension of some operations due to power shortages." || Environmental | Air & Climate | Energy demand and consumption -> "Security of electricity supply has also helped confidence. All mining companies are receiving enough electricity to operate at full capacity after months of power rationing." | "Companies such as FQM and KCM had earlier announced the suspension of some operations due to power shortages." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "The new investment plans are partly based on projected stability of the mining tax system, which government has promised after earlier uncertainly. It changed the mining tax regime three times in 2015 alone." | "Confidence in the sector has also been boosted by government's about-turn both on taxes — tax hikes for copper miners were abandoned last year — and on its plans to withhold more than $500m in Vat refunds owed to mining companies." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Corporate transparency and reporting -> "In 2014, government withheld Vat refunds for companies that failed to produce import certificates from countries that bought their copper. The certificate requirement aimed to force transparency among mining companies and help improve government tax collection."

title: Zambia : There is more to mining than digging minerals, Gift Mwaula tells us about his work at the Geological Survey Department
impact_evidence: Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Mining permitting and licensing practices -> ""Every single miner operating in Zambia is required to pass through the Geological Survey Department before they can export their minerals. We have to verify their minerals, and issue them with analysis and evaluation certificates."" || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> ""A lot of times we are not able to go the mines due to lack of funds so we end up relying on these samples that the miners bring by themselves, and occasionally those samples are not representative of what they want to export. The risk then is that the sample being given to us is not entirely accurate."" || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Corporate transparency and reporting -> "By analysing minerals in a laboratory to understand their content, the precise royalties and payments required can be accurately calculated."
impact_level2: Regulation, Compliance & Institutions || Regulation, Compliance & Institutions || Transparency & Accountability
